questions.question: Akbar was born in _____ in Amarkot Fort in present-day Sind.
questions.explanation: Akbar was born in 1542 while his father Humayun was in exile in Sind and Rajasthan.

questions.question: Akbar's full name was Jalaluddin Muhammad _____.
questions.explanation: Akbar's full name was Jalaluddin Muhammad Akbar, meaning 'Akbar the Great'.

questions.question: Akbar was brought up by his paternal uncle _____.
questions.explanation: While Humayun was in exile, young Akbar was brought up by his paternal uncle Kamran.

questions.question: Akbar's coronation took place in _____ in Punjab in 1556.
questions.explanation: When Humayun died, Akbar was at Kalanaur in Punjab, and his coronation was held there in 1556.

questions.question: _____ served as the regent of Akbar from 1556 to 1560.
questions.explanation: Bairam Khan, Humayun's favourite and confidant, served as regent of Akbar from 1556 to 1560.

questions.question: Bairam Khan held the title of _____.
questions.explanation: Bairam Khan held the prestigious title of Khan-i-Khanan during his regency.

questions.question: _____, Akbar's half-brother, revolted from Kabul.
questions.explanation: Mirza Hakim, Akbar's half-brother, revolted from Kabul and posed a challenge to Akbar's authority.

questions.question: Hemu was struck by an arrow in his _____ during the Second Battle of Panipat.
questions.explanation: During the Second Battle of Panipat, Hemu was struck by an arrow in his eye and rendered unconscious, leading to the Afghan army's panic and retreat.

questions.question: Adil Shah was defeated and killed in Bengal by _____ in April 1557.
questions.explanation: After the Second Battle of Panipat, Adil Shah was also defeated and killed in Bengal by Khizr Khan in April 1557.

questions.question: Bairam Khan's son became the influential noble _____.
questions.explanation: After Bairam Khan was killed near Ahmedabad, his son became the influential noble Abdur Rahim Khan-i-Khana, one of the Navratnas.

questions.question: In 1564, Akbar conquered _____ where Rani Durgawati's kingdom was annexed.
questions.explanation: In 1564, Akbar conquered Gondwana, where Rani Durgawati ruled. The kingdom was conquered and annexed.

questions.question: _____ of Amber was the first Rajput to enter into an alliance with Akbar.
questions.explanation: Raja Bharmal of Amber was the first Rajput chief to enter into a matrimonial alliance with Akbar.

questions.question: The Mughal empire was divided into _____ subas by Akbar, which later grew to twenty.
questions.explanation: Akbar initially divided his empire into twelve provinces (subas), which later grew to twenty with the addition of Ahmadnagar, Berar, and Khandesh.

questions.question: The _____ was the officer in-charge of the royal Karkhanas.
questions.explanation: The Mir Saman was the officer in-charge of royal Karkhanas (manufacturing) in Akbar's central administration.

questions.question: In the Mansabdari system, 'Mansab' literally means _____ or rank.
questions.explanation: The literal meaning of 'Mansab' is office or rank. Every officer or noble in Akbar's administration was a Mansabdar.

questions.question: Raja Todar Mal introduced bamboo sticks called _____ with iron rings for land measurement.
questions.explanation: Raja Todar Mal introduced bamboo sticks called jarib with iron rings, replacing rope which changed in wet and dry conditions.

questions.question: The dahsaal system was introduced in _____ and fixed revenue based on the average of the last 10 years.
questions.explanation: The dahsaal system, introduced in 1580, fixed the revenue after assessing the average produce and prices of the last 10 years.

questions.question: Land not cultivated for more than 5 years was classified as _____.
questions.explanation: Under Akbar's revenue system, land was classified into four categories: Polaj, Parauti, Chachar, and Banjar (not cultivated for more than 5 years).

questions.question: The gold coin during Akbar's reign was called _____.
questions.explanation: During Akbar's reign, the gold coin was called mohur, the silver coin was called rupee, and the copper coin was called dam or paisa.

questions.question: Akbar suggested a new religious path called _____.
questions.explanation: After discussions with scholars of various faiths, Akbar suggested Din-i-Ilahi (religion of one God), which was like a Sufi order.

questions.question: In 1575, Akbar built _____ as his new capital city.
questions.explanation: Akbar built Fatehpur Sikri near Agra as his new capital in 1575, selecting the site because the Sufi saint Salim Chisti lived there.

questions.question: The _____ was the Hall of Worship built by Akbar for religious discussions.
questions.explanation: Akbar built the Ibadat Khana (Hall of Worship) where he invited scholars of different faiths for religious discussions.

questions.question: Abul Fazl wrote the _____, which was an official biographical account of Akbar's life.
questions.explanation: Abul Fazl wrote the Akbarnama, commissioned by Akbar. It took seven years to complete and contained paintings.

questions.question: The _____ is the highest gateway in India, built at Fatehpur Sikri.
questions.explanation: The Buland Darwaza was built at Fatehpur Sikri to commemorate Akbar's victory over Gujarat. It is the highest gateway in India and one of the biggest in the world.

questions.question: Akbar raised the minimum marriage age to _____ for girls and _____ for boys.
questions.explanation: As part of his social reforms, Akbar discouraged child marriage and raised the minimum marriage age to 14 for girls and 16 for boys.
